title: Anthropic API outage — recurring billing exhaustion pattern
problemStatement: AInchors has experienced two separate overnight outages caused by Anthropic API credit exhaustion within the first 3 days of operation. Each time, the platform went silent with no fallback completing successfully.
rootCause: 1. No automated spend monitoring with early warning. 2. Fallback chain was not validated on startup — Ollama auth was missing. 3. No standby mode banner to inform Ken on reconnect.
workaround: Manual top-up by Ken. Validated by running zsh scripts/validate-fallback-chain.sh post-recovery.

actions: 3-tier credit alert system ($50/$25/$10) — Ken + Angie notified (CHG-0032)
Daily burn alert at $40 USD threshold (CHG-0050)
outage-handler.sh — auto-detects API failure, activates standby mode, alerts Ken (CHG-0058)
validate-fallback-chain.sh — tests all chain links including Haiku T2 and gemma4:e2b (CHG-0058)

kedb: Billing exhaustion → API 429/401 → check console.anthropic.com → top up → re-run validate-fallback-chain.sh
preventionMeasures: Burn alert + credit alerts prevent recurrence. If balance <$50 Ken+Angie alerted. Standby mode auto-activates if API goes down.
closureCondition: No further billing outages for 30 days. Monitor via burn alert compliance.
lessons: Set credit alerts before go-live, not after first outage
Validate fallback chain on every gateway start, not just on incident
Overnight outages are silent without a standby banner
